event: neonate delivered spontaneously at full term
parents were from Somalia and were nonconsanguineous
no family history of metabolic disease
neonate’s male sibling was developmentally normal
admitted to the neonatal intensive care unit for a sepsis workup
amino acid analysis of the cerebral spinal fluid and plasma was requested
liver studies and an ammonia level were normal
MR imaging of the brain was performed
CSF and plasma amino acid analysis revealed glycine levels
follow-up weekly amino acid analysis revealed overall decreased plasma glycine concentrations
DNA testing demonstrated a homozygous missense mutation
ongoing medical issues including feeding problems
